SECTOR:
[Technology, Software & Media] 
This sector encompasses global leaders in consumer electronics, cloud computing, e-commerce, enterprise software, and digital entertainment (e.g., AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA, NFLX, DIS, ADBE, CRM, ORCL). Key drivers include user growth metrics, cloud revenue, AI integration trends, and consumer discretionary spending.
TARGET: This memory file serves as a domain knowledge base to retrieve events relevant to stock price trends. By analyzing how past events impacted historical prices, the agent learns structural patterns, filters market noise, and identifies the most critical information needed for accurate future price predictions in this sector.
MEMORY: Macro/Liquidity Dominance: Fed policy and sector-wide liquidity override idiosyncratic data; institutional risk-parity liquidation ignores positive catalysts until cycles conclude.

The Funding-First Hierarchy: In capital-intensive tech, "Balance Sheet Stability" and "Funding Certainty" supersede "Operational Progress" or "AI Growth" narratives; until debt is refinanced, positive news acts as an institutional exit-liquidity window.

Institutional De-Risking Protocol: During "valuation-reset" or "litigation-overhang" cycles, positive analyst upgrades and project approvals are treated as noise; institutional selling persists until a concrete, audited funding solution is disclosed.

Capitulation & Sentiment Inversion: In deeply oversold regimes (>15% drawdown), analyst downgrades act as the final capitulation signal, while negative news becomes a contrarian floor for institutional accumulation.

The Foundry-to-Cloud Validation: Positive CapEx signals from upstream semiconductor foundries (e.g., TSMC) serve as the primary proxy for project viability, overriding company-specific "litigation" or "funding" doubts.

The Litigation/Insider Multiplier: In post-shock environments, class-action noise and insider selling are disproportionately amplified, serving as "credibility anchors" that decouple the stock from broader sector rallies.
Structural Valuation Buffers: Assets tied to mission-critical infrastructure or high-margin non-AI mandates possess a "valuation floor" that provides resilience against analyst-downgrade shocks.

The "Sell-the-News" Liquidity Trap: In high-volatility environments, institutional holders utilize positive fundamental catalysts to execute exit orders, resulting in price drops despite positive headlines.

Industry Event Alpha: Major sector conferences (e.g., CES) act as exogenous sentiment resets capable of overriding idiosyncratic negative news by shifting focus to long-term infrastructure narratives.

The CapEx-Sensitivity Contagion: When sector bellwethers (e.g., MSFT) report a disconnect between massive CapEx and revenue growth, companies with similar debt-funded AI infrastructure profiles suffer immediate "sympathy" sell-offs, regardless of their own product-specific positive news.

Regulatory/Litigation Ceiling Effect: During periods of high-intensity legal news cycle (e.g., class-action filings), positive fundamental catalysts (product launches, partnerships) are systematically discounted by the market, functioning only as liquidity windows for institutional exits rather than price-appreciation drivers.
MISSING: Capital Reliability Index: Tracks debt-to-EBITDA, CDS spreads, and financing-partner stability to quantify infrastructure project-collapse risk.
Sector Liquidity & Positioning Flow: Monitors institutional rotation, risk-parity constraints, and holiday-period volume to identify liquidity-driven price traps.
Sentiment-Momentum Decay Model: Measures the velocity at which sector-wide news sentiment is neutralized by valuation-reset narratives post-earnings.
Capitulation & Support Thresholds: Tracks analyst downgrade density and short-interest coverage against long-term moving averages to identify structural bottoms.
Macro-Beta Decoupling Metric: Quantifies idiosyncratic valuation-reset phases by tracking shifts in sector-wide correlation to index benchmarks and event-cycle volatility.

Financing-Gap Resolution Tracker: A signal that maps the delta between "failed financing deals" and "new capital commitments," specifically tracking the creditworthiness of new partners versus the previous failed entity.

Institutional "Upgrade-Reaction" Sentiment: A metric that distinguishes between "buying-side" volume and "liquidity-provision" volume following major analyst upgrades to identify if an upgrade is being used as an exit window.

Litigation-Noise Sensitivity Index: A quantitative measure of how much price-action variance is explained by legal solicitation news (class-action lawsuits) versus fundamental business news, providing a "distraction-to-value" ratio.
CapEx-Demand Correlation Coefficient: A metric measuring the sensitivity of specific cloud providers to the CapEx guidance of major foundry partners (TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix).

Litigation-Noise Decay Index: A measure of the "news-velocity" of legal headlines, tracking whether fresh articles contain new information or are merely re-reporting existing lawsuits, helping to identify when "litigation fatigue" is reached.

Institutional Short-Interest Coverage Ratio: A real-time tracking of short-interest levels relative to average daily volume, used to identify the potential for "short-squeeze" rallies when positive macro-catalysts (like TSMC earnings) occur in a depressed market.

Geopolitical-Macro Sensitivity Score: A quantitative filter that measures how much of a stock's volatility is tied to specific geopolitical risk premiums (e.g., tariff threats, regional conflicts) versus idiosyncratic fundamental performance.

Short-Covering Velocity Metric: A real-time indicator tracking the volume of short-position closures during "relief rally" windows to differentiate between genuine institutional accumulation and temporary algorithmic short-covering.

Narrative-Decay Monitoring: A tracker for the "half-life" of negative sentiment cycles (e.g., litigation, downgrades) to determine when the market has fully "digested" negative news and is primed for a contrarian reversal.

Analyst-Sentiment "Capitulation Density" Metric: A quantitative threshold measuring the number of analyst downgrades occurring within a 48-hour window relative to the stock's 30-day volatility, used to signal an imminent "short-squeeze" or "reversal" bottom.

"Litigation-Fatigue" Decay Coefficient: A real-time tracker of the price impact of legal news; as the same lawsuit is reported multiple times without new disclosures, this coefficient measures the declining sensitivity of the stock price to that specific legal narrative.

"Institutional-Accumulation" Volume Divergence: A signal that tracks price-action against volume during negative-news cycles; if the stock price remains stable or ticks up during a "barrage" of negative news, it indicates institutional absorption (buying) that precedes a rally.

Institutional "Exit-Volume" Tracker: A metric that compares the volume of "up-ticks" on positive news against the net change in institutional ownership to identify if an event is triggering accumulation or distribution.

Debt-Structure Transparency Index: A quantitative score measuring the frequency and depth of management’s disclosures regarding debt-refinancing and CapEx-funding, used to predict when the "Funding-Certainty Gap" is likely to close.
Insider-Sentiment Sensitivity Score: A measure of the correlation between insider-selling volume and retail-sentiment decline, specifically during periods of high litigation or regulatory scrutiny.

"News-to-Liquidity" Conversion Ratio: A metric that calculates the ratio of price movement to volume during the first 60 minutes of a positive news release, helping to determine if the market is "buying the news" or "selling into the liquidity."

"AI-ROI Disconnect" Correlation Metric: A signal tracking the historical correlation between a company’s CapEx-to-Revenue ratio and sector-wide sentiment toward AI-infrastructure investments, used to predict how much a company will suffer during "CapEx-anxiety" market cycles.

"Litigation-to-Catalyst" Imbalance Tracker: A quantitative tool that compares the volume of "legal-news-cycle" mentions against "product-launch-news" mentions, identifying when a stock is trapped in a "negative-noise" cycle that effectively neutralizes fundamental valuation.

"Sympathy-Selling" Beta: A metric that quantifies how much of a stock's intraday volatility is caused by the earnings or news flow of larger, correlated industry peers (e.g., ORCL's dependence on MSFT's cloud sentiment).
